# Generated by ollama (mistral)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build

on: [push, pull_request]

jobs:
  build:
    name: Build
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      build_status: '${{ steps.build.outputs.build_status }}'

    inputs:
      build_command: ''

    steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v2

    - name: Set up JDK 11
      uses: actions/setup-java@v2
      with:
        java-version: '11'
        distribution: 'adopt'

    - name: Build with custom command
      id: build
      shell: bash
      run: |
        eval $(ssh-agent -s)
        echo "${{ secrets.SSH_KEY }}" | tr -d '\r' > ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
        chmod 600 ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
        ssh-add ~/.ssh/id_rsa
        mkdir -p ~/.ssh
        chmod 700 ~/.ssh
        ssh-keyscan github.com >> ~/.ssh/known_hosts
        git config --global user.email "action@github.com"
        git config --global user.name "GitHub Action Bot"
        cd ${{ github.workspace }}
        echo 'Running custom build command: ${{ inputs.build_command }}'
        eval ${{ inputs.build_command }}
        echo 'Build completed.'
        if [ $? -eq 0 ]; then
          echo 'Build status: success' >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        else
          echo 'Build status: failure' >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
        fi
